steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "As nanotechnology evolves, medical technology ______ to evolve as well." The "as" clause describes an ongoing and future process (evolution of nanotechnology). The main clause must express what will happen during that ongoing process, so it needs a future-oriented verb form.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — had likely been continuing: past perfect progressive — places the action before a reference point in the past, which contradicts the forward-looking "as nanotechnology evolves." ✗
  • Choice B — will likely continue: simple future, correctly expressing what is expected to happen as nanotechnology continues to develop. ✓
  • Choice C — would likely have continued: past conditional perfect — implies the action was expected to have already happened under some unfulfilled condition, not a future expectation. ✗
  • Choice D — had likely continued: past perfect — places the action as already completed before a past reference point, which conflicts with "as nanotechnology evolves." ✗
That matches Choice B — will likely continue.
